# CASMIM — Cellular Automata and Social Mirror Identities SARS Simulation

> CASMIM is one of the first social-network-based infectious disease simulation systems in Taiwan, combining cellular automata with social mirror identities models to simulate SARS transmission dynamics and evaluate public health policy interventions.

## Authors

- Name: Chung-Yuan Huang (黃崇源) (corresponding author)
- Title: Full Professor, Department of CSIE, Chang Gung University
- Email: gscott@mail.cgu.edu.tw
- ORCID: https://orcid.org/0000-0002-8680-6755
- Google Scholar: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=0klfzfAAAAAJ&hl=en
- Homepage: https://canslab1.github.io/

- Name: Chuen-Tsai Sun
- Affiliation: Department of Computer Science, National Yang Ming Chiao Tung University, Taiwan

- Name: Ji-Lung Hsieh
- Affiliation: Graduate Institute of Journalism, National Taiwan University, Taiwan

- Name: Yi-Ming Arthur Chen
- Affiliation: Department of Information Management, National Central University, Taiwan

- Name: Holin Lin
- Affiliation: Department of Sociology, National Taiwan University, Taiwan

## Software

- Name: CASMIM
- Language: Python
- License: MIT
- GitHub: https://github.com/canslab1/CASMIM
- README: https://canslab1.github.io/CASMIM/

## Publication

Huang, C.-Y., Sun, C.-T., Hsieh, J.-L., Chen, Y.-M. A., & Lin, H. (2005). A Novel Small-World Model: Using Social Mirror Identities for Epidemic Simulations. SIMULATION, 81(10), 671-699.
DOI: https://doi.org/10.1177/0037549705061519

## Lab

- CANS Lab (Complex Adaptive Networks and Systems Laboratory)
- Chang Gung University, Department of CSIE
- Website: https://canslab1.github.io/
